title: Scaling Feast
description: An in depth, progressive extension of Minecraft's hunger system.
body:
**Psst! Remember CurseForge/Modrinth/GitHub is the *only* place you should be getting Scaling Feast from. Not on CurseForge? Don't know how to get there? Don't worry, you're covered. Just click [here](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/scaling-feast).**   ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/refs/heads/master/images/sflogo2.png) ### This mod requires [AppleCore](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/applecore), which as of writing, is NOT available on Modrinth! ***In particular:*** **Versions 1.3.3 and below require AppleCore 3.2.0 or higher** **Version 1.4.0 requires AppleCore 3.3.0 or higher** **Version 1.5.0 and higher requires AppleCore 3.4.0 or higher**     [![ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/img/githubbutton_sm.svg)](https://ko-fi.com/X8X5G4LPG) [![Patreon](https://i.imgur.com/JkRflNx.png)](https://www.patreon.com/join/Yeelp) (Supporting on Ko-fi or Patreon helps me develop mods full time!) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/750481601107853373?style=flat&logo=discord&logoColor=white&color=5662f6)](https://discord.gg/hwzWdXQ) ![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/yeelp/Scaling-Feast) [![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/yeelp/Scaling-Feast)](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/issues)   Scaling Feast is an progressive extension of Minecraft's base hunger system. This mod aims to gradually grant players the benefits of deeper hunger reserves over time. Mods which grant the player an unlimited saturation cap may not recognize that by doing so, they grant the player easy access to an extended form of health regeneration stronger than the base game's own Regeneration III (With saturation, the player gains health at one full heart per second, compared to Regeneration III at 0.8 hearts per second). Scaling Feast allows players to work towards this, should they choose to. Scaling Feast pairs well with other food mods, like [Pam's HarvestCraft](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/pams-harvestcraft). This mod is not an addon to SilentChaos512's [Scaling Health](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/scaling-health), although it was inspired by it. ## Features This mod adds 5 obtainable items and a new ore. ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/refs/heads/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/heartyshank.png) The **Hearty Shank** is the item players will craft and consume to gain extra hunger. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/ironapple.png?raw=true) The **Iron Apple** is a crafting component for the Hearty Shank, but can also be consumed to regenerate hunger slowly. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/ironapple.png?raw=true) An uncraftable enchanted variation exists that regenerates hunger at a faster rate, while also reducing the rate at which a player's exhaustion increases, effectively making each hunger point last longer. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/heartyfeast.png?raw=true) A cake-like block called a **Hearty Feast** can be obtained with the Hearty Shank. This meat pie block restores a percentage of your max hunger every time you eat a slice, The higher your max hunger, the more it restores! ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/exhausting_potato.png?raw=true) The **Exhausting Potato**, made with the new ore, which reduces your hunger by 4 when eating it. Yes, that's all it does. Well, you can use it as a Nether Wart alternative to make Awkward Potions. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/exhausting_apple.png?raw=true) The **Exhausting Apple**, made with the new ore, which fully restores your hunger and saturation, but also decreases your max hunger. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/exhausting_ingot.png?raw=true) ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/src/main/resources/assets/scalingfeast/textures/items/exhausting_nugget.png?raw=true) The new ore, **Exhausting Ore**, can be normally found in places that are very hot or very cold. It has ingot, nugget and block forms and has various uses. This mod also improves the HUD, with various configuration options. ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/v1.3.0/images/spawnshanks.png) Coloured hunger shanks will be drawn on top of the vanilla icons to display how much 'extra' hunger you have. Coloured outlines will also be drawn to show your additional saturation (and a bolder outline will be drawn to show you maximum hunger). There are configuration options to change the colours used, stop drawing the saturation altogether. You can also change the information that is displayed to the right. You can change the amount of hunger you start with too. Maybe you never want to sprint... ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/v1.3.0/images/littlespawnshanks.png) Or maybe you never want hunger to be an issue... ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/v1.3.0/images/lostaspawnshanks.png) The choice is yours! There is also a configurable penalty for starving. By starving, you'll notice the bold max hunger outline change colour. ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/master/images/starvealittle.png) Starve some more, and the colour will change some more until it becomes dark red. ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/master/images/starvealot.png) Be wary! At this point, taking damage from starvation one more time will cause your max hunger to drop by a configurable amount, resetting this counter in the process. ![](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/master/images/starveconsequence.png) This penalty is very configurable and can be disabled altogether. Mobs can also damage a player's food stats (configurable and can be disabled). That is, Zombies (and every other non-player mob) can decrease your hunger and your health in a single attack (without adding potion effects!) Dynamic Starvation! For every food point you would've lost while starving, you'll take extra damage the next time you take starvation damage! This is particularly nasty with the Famine enchantment (explained below) or the ability for mobs to damage food stats as explained above. Exhaustion Scaling is a new feature that causing the player to lose hunger faster when mining blocks depending on how long it takes them to break it. This mod also adds a few enchantments and potion effects to take advantage of the deeper hunger reserves. **Gluttony** is a chestplate treasure enchantment that increases the food value of food items you eat. **Fasting** is a chestplate enchantment that reduces your exhaustion rate by 10% per level. **Famine** is a sword enchantment that cripples targets and rapidly drains hunger from them (if they are a player). **Eternal Feast** is a treasure sword enchantment that restores some food when you kill an entity. **Curse of Deprivation** is a treasure curse that makes a player take starvation damage faster. **Curse of Laziness** is a treasure curse that halves a player's total exhaustion (default 4 -> 2) **Curse of Sensitivity** is a treasure curse that gives a Soft Stomach debuff for overeating (you can actually enable this globally without this curse enchant if you want). Potions in this mod are brewed from a Thick Potion (Glowstone base). Like vanilla brewing, secondary ingredients brew Mundane Potions (except for the Exhausting Potato, which brews an Awkward Potion) ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/metabolism.png?raw=true) **Metabolism** is a potion effect that can be brewed from a Hearty Shank or obtained by eating an Iron Apple. It regenerates hunger slowly (At exactly the same rate as health regeneration at the same level). ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/bloated.png?raw=true) **Bloated** is a potion effect that is essentially absorption for hunger. Also obtained from Iron Apples. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/bloatedshanks.png?raw=true) Much like hunger, it has coloured overlays as well and uses different colours depending on how much bloated hunger you have ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/lotsbloated.png?raw=true) ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/hungerplus.png?raw=true) ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/hungerminus.png?raw=true) **Hunger Plus/Minus** are potion effects that increase/decrease max hunger. Hunger Minus can be obtained from Molten Exhaustion (Requires Tinker's Construct) or brewed from an Exhausting Nugget. Hunger Minus potions can be inverted into Hunger Plus potions with a Fermented Spider Eye. **Deficiency** is an instant potion effect that acts as the opposite to vanilla's Saturation. It can be brewed with an Exhausting Potato. If Scaling Feast finds Saturation potions registered (like from Potion Core), Scaling Feast will add inversion recipes to invert Deficiency potions into Saturation potions. Scaling Feast on its own does not add Saturation potions. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/ironstomach.png?raw=true) **Iron Stomach** is a potion effect similar to the Fasting enchantment. It reduces the rate of exhaustion by 20% per level. It can only be obtained by eating an enchanted Iron Apple. ![](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/blob/master/images/softstomach.png?raw=true) **Soft Stomach** is a potion effect that does the opposite of Iron Stomach (Increases exhaustion rate by 20% per level). It can be obtained from Molten Exhaustion (Tinker's Construct required). If you want more information about a particular topic, including crafting recipes, and more detailed explanations, [check out the (still incomplete) wiki](https://github.com/yeelp/Scaling-Feast/wiki) (linked in the page header as well)! With Tinker's Construct and Construct's Armory, the Exhausting metal can be used as a tinkering material. Lastly, this mod has some integration with Spice of Life, and Spice of Life Carrot Edition! You can increase or decrease your max hunger depending on how varied your diet is! **Psst! Remember CurseForge/Modrinth/GitHub is the *only* place you should be getting Scaling Feast from. Not on CurseForge? Don't know how to get there? Don't worry, you're covered. Just click [here](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/scaling-feast).**     
FAQ

1. Can you backport this mod to [Pre 1.12.2 Minecraft Version]?

No. I would like to move forward, not backwards in versions.

2. When will you port this mod to [Newer Minecraft Version]?

It is something I would like to do, but I have no timeline set. Once this mod is ported to a newer Minecraft version, versions of the mod for older Minecraft versions will only receive patches for critical bugs. No features will be backported (subject to my final decision).

3. I'm having problems with this mod!/This mod doesn't work with [Mod Name]!

Please open an issue on this mod's issue tracker, following the bug report issue template. Problems reported to this Curse Forge page have a high chance of being overlooked.

4. This mod definitely needs [Feature or Idea].

Please open an issue on this mod's issue tracker, following the feature request issue template. Please keep in mind that bug fixes take precedent over feature requests.

5. Can I use this mod in a modpack? Can I stream Minecraft content with this mod installed, or otherwise create any consumable media using this mod?

YES! I strongly encourage modpack authors to  fiddle with this mod! It'll help it gain some exposure and probably help squash a handful of bugs in the process. Everybody wins! The same goes to streamers. Modpack authors can send me links to their packs if they wish. I'd like to see how Scaling Feast gets used. It could help with development decisions (Heck, I might even play/stream the pack).

6.  Can I host this mod on [Webpage]?

NO, NO, NO! CurseForge, Modrinth and GitHub are currently the home for Scaling Feast. If you still believe that this mod should be hosted on that webpage, contact me, and we'll discuss it. By contacting me in this manner, for this purpose, you agree that you give me permission to message you at any time, at any frequency, for any reason regarding this mod or the page that you suggested this mod be hosted on.

**Psst! Remember CurseForge/Modrinth/GitHub is the *only* place you should be getting Scaling Feast from. Not on CurseForge? Don't know how to get there? Don't worry, you're covered. Just click [here](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/scaling-feast).**     # Discord There's now a Discord server for my mods! Come hang out and chat about development, features, or really whatever you want! The Discord badge above contains a link for joining. Just click that.
